messages.content:
HOW YOUR CLAIM BECOMES A NUMBER (the perturbation contract):
- You never state € impacts. You emit machine-readable ops; the engine computes
  ΔTAM/ΔSAM/ΔYAM by re-running the real funnel. Any € figure you write in prose
  will be stripped.
- Ops: {nodeId, op: "set"|"scale"|"add", value} rewrite one leaf's value;
  {nodeId, op: "exclude"} removes a dimension cell from the venture's reachable
  scope ("unavailable to us" — foreclosure), which is different from shrinking it.
- Ops apply in array order. Ratio nodes must stay inside [0,1]; tamBase must stay > 0.
- Only FUNNEL-ACTIVE nodes may be perturbed (marked in the ledger you receive).
  shape.cagr and shape.cr3 are context — a demand-pace or concentration risk must
  be translated into the funnel nodes it would actually move (tamBase level,
  serviceableFactor reach, obtainableFactor pace, or a share).
- Sibling shares are NOT re-normalized when you move one share (documented v1
  simplification). If you mean a mix-SHIFT, perturb multiple siblings; if you
  mean "this slice is smaller in absolute terms", one op is correct.
- Perturbing an assumption leaf overrides whatever the user set: your claim is
  about the world, not about their slider.

Fix the hypothesis's targetNodes/perturbation so it passes validation WITHOUT changing the claim's meaning. If the claim cannot be expressed against funnel-active nodes, translate it into the funnel nodes it would actually move.

FUNNEL-ACTIVE NODES:
tamBase (EUR_M, value 300)
geo.DE (ratio, value 0.5)
geo.NL (ratio, value 0.28)
geo.PL (ratio, value 0.07)
geo.CH (ratio, value 0.08)
geo.CZ (ratio, value 0.02)
geo.AT (ratio, value 0.02)
geo.other (ratio, value 0.03)
seg.hyperscale (ratio, value 0.44)
seg.colocation (ratio, value 0.18)
seg.enterprise (ratio, value 0.29)
seg.edge (ratio, value 0.05)
seg.telecom (ratio, value 0.04)
cust.operator-large (ratio, value 0.4)
cust.operator-mid (ratio, value 0.25)
cust.oem (ratio, value 0.22)
cust.distributor (ratio, value 0.13)
serviceableFactor (ratio, value 0.55)
obtainableFactor (ratio, value 0.01)

HYPOTHESIS:
{
 "id": "red-en18031-wireless-sensor-gate",
 "title": "RED cybersecurity delegated act (EU 2022/30, applicable since 1 Aug 2025): one WiFi sensor port drags the PDU into EN 18031 conformity and adds two quarters to launch",
 "narrative": "If the intelligent PDU ships with any radio — WiFi management fallback, BLE commissioning, wireless environmental sensors — it is radio equipment, and since 1 August 2025 the Radio Equipment Directive's cybersecurity delegated act applies in full. Conformity runs against EN 18031-1/-2, which are harmonized only WITH RESTRICTIONS: where the restrictions bite (e.g., password/access-control configurations), self-declaration is unavailable and a notified body must issue an EU-type examination certificate. For a pre-launch team this is not a 2027 problem like the CRA — it is a today problem that gates the very first shipment. The obtainableFactor's 1% Year-1 anchor already sits on a corrected, fragile benchmark; a 4–6 month notified-body queue on top of an already pre-let, slow-procurement market compresses the sellable window from 12 months to 6–8.",
 "category": "execution",
 "targetNodes": [
  "obtainableFactor"
 ],
 "mechanism": [
  {
   "step": "trigger",
   "text": "The launch configuration includes a radio (wireless sensors / BLE commissioning), pulling the product under the RED cybersecurity delegated act — in force since 1 Aug 2025, i.e., before the venture's first shipment",
   "refs": [
    "profile:Product",
    "profile:Stage"
   ]
  },
  {
   "step": "propagation",
   "text": "EN 18031's harmonization restrictions force notified-body involvement for common access-control configurations; certificate lead time lands mid-window, and the market's own procurement clock (gear locked 12–24+ months pre-fit-out, few contestable standalone tenders) means a late start forfeits most Year-1 bake-offs",
   "refs": [
    "digest:refine-demand-discontinuity.prelet-pipeline-lockout#c0",
    "digest:refine-competitive-foreclosure.bundling-eliminates-bakeoffs-year1#c2",
    "ledger:obtainableFactor"
   ]
  },
  {
   "step": "model-impact",
   "text": "The Year-1 obtainable share, whose 1% value already rests on a benchmark-period correction and a shaky Tractian comparable, is cut roughly in half by a shortened selling window",
   "refs": [
    "ledger:obtainableFactor",
    "digest:obtainable-benchmark#c5",
    "digest:refine-execution-window.som-window-semantics-mismatch#c4"
   ]
  }
 ],
 "whyMissable": "RED reads as a law about radios, and this is a power-distribution company; the risk hides in an accessory feature (a wireless sensor accessory or BLE setup app) that the electrical engineering team considers trivial. The compliance plan covers LVD/EMC/EN 62368-1 for the PDU itself; nobody owns the delegated act triggered by the cheapest component on the BOM.",
 "expectedObviousness": "low",
 "falsifier": "Confirmation that the launch SKU is wired-only (no radio in any shipping configuration), or an EN 18031 assessment showing the chosen access-control design falls entirely within the harmonized (self-declaration) scope with certificate in hand before first customer ship.",
 "likelihood": {
  "value": 0.35,
  "rationale": "Conditional on product configuration: wireless sensing/commissioning is common in current intelligent-PDU designs, and the EN 18031 restriction traps are documented, but a wired-only launch SKU fully avoids the gate.",
  "basis": "judgment"
 },
 "perturbation": [
  {
   "nodeId": "obtainableFactor",
   "op": "scale",
   "value": 0.5,
   "note": "Selling window compressed from 12 to ~6–8 months by notified-body lead time"
  }
 ],
 "indicators": [
  {
   "signal": "EN 18031 harmonization restrictions status (whether the OJ listing's restrictions are lifted or extended)",
   "where": "EU Official Journal harmonized-standards citations under RED; NANDO database queue times for RED cybersecurity notified bodies",
   "threshold": "Notified-body lead quotes >4 months, or restrictions extended into 2026",
   "updates": "increases"
  },
  {
   "signal": "Own-product radio scope decision and test-house booking",
   "where": "Internal launch BOM review; test-house (TÜV/DEKRA) quotation and slot calendar for EN 18031",
   "threshold": "Radio in launch SKU with no notified-body slot booked 9 months pre-launch",
   "updates": "increases"
  }
 ],
 "mitigations": [
  {
   "action": "Run an immediate RED applicability screen on every launch configuration and get a written EN 18031 gap assessment from a notified body",
   "type": "information",
   "voiNodeId": "obtainableFactor",
   "note": "Converts an unknown certification clock into a dated one"
  },
  {
   "action": "De-content the launch SKU to wired-only (Ethernet/serial), shipping wireless sensing as a post-certification accessory",
   "type": "operational",
   "voiNodeId": null,
   "note": "Removes the gate from the critical path entirely"
  }
 ],
 "lens": "regulatory-gauntlet",
 "riskId": "risk.regulatory-gauntlet.red-en18031-wireless-sensor-gate"
}

VALIDATION ERROR:
[
  {
    "code": "invalid_value",
    "values": [
      "error",
      "risk"
    ],
    "path": [
      0,
      "resolution"
    ],
    "message": "Invalid option: expected one of \"error\"|\"risk\""
  }
]

Emit the corrected hypothesis JSON (same shape).

NANDO database queue times for RED cybersecurity notified bodies","threshold":"Notified-body lead quotes >4 months, or restrictions extended into 2026","updates":"increases"},{"signal":"Own-product radio scope decision and test-house booking","where":"Internal launch BOM review; test-house (TÜV/DEKRA) quotation and slot calendar for EN 18031","threshold":"Radio in launch SKU with no notified-body slot booked 9 months pre-launch","updates":"increases"}],"mitigations":[{"action":"Run an immediate RED applicability screen on every launch configuration and get a written EN 18031 gap assessment from a notified body","type":"information","voiNodeId":"obtainableFactor","note":"Converts an unknown certification clock into a dated one"},{"action":"De-content the launch SKU to wired-only (Ethernet/serial), shipping wireless sensing as a post-certification accessory","type":"operational","voiNodeId":null,"note":"Removes the gate from the critical path entirely"}]}
